script: Welcome to Newton's Laws of Motion. These three fundamental laws explain how objects move and interact with forces in our world.
The first law, called the Law of Inertia, tells us that an object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion keeps moving at the same speed and direction, unless acted on by an external force.
Newton's second law explains how force, mass, and acceleration are related. It says force equals mass times acceleration, meaning heavier objects need more force to speed up.
The third law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. When one object pushes another, the second pushes back with the same force in the opposite direction.
To summarize, Newton's first law describes inertia, the second law connects force, mass, and acceleration, and the third law explains action and reaction forces working in pairs.
